source: Neural Ranking Models with Weak Supervision

concepts.finding: Train neural ranking models using BM25 scores as weak supervision. Pre-training on billions of weakly labeled instances + fine-tuning on small labeled data. Achieves 13% and 35% MAP improvement over BM25 on Robust and ClueWeb. Key: pairwise ranking objectives outperform pointwise; letting network learn representations generalizes better.
concepts.implication: Weak supervision can bootstrap keyword-intent clustering: use heuristic/rule-based keyword-intent assignments as weak labels. Pairwise training objectives ('intent A > intent B for keyword K') generalize better than pointwise classification of weak labels.
concepts.open_question: What weak supervision signals (search logs, taxonomy rules, LLM-based labeling) are most effective for bootstrapping keyword-intent models?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A neural ranker pre-trained on weak keyword-intent labels and fine-tuned on curated data outperforms a model trained on curated data only, especially for tail keywords.
